section_title: Client Due Diligence: General Requirements and CDD for Individuals

content_blocks.text:
#### 10.3.4 Verification of Personal Presence (Paragraph 4.2.4)

The Company shall ensure that the client whose credentials are being furnished is the same individual actually accessing the Company's application and personally initiating the account creation process.

The authenticity of such access and personal presence shall be established by:

(a) Capturing a live photograph of the client; and

(b) Employing liveness detection technology to verify the client's physical presence at the time of onboarding.

Failure of liveness detection, or evidence that the credentials are being furnished by a person other than the actual client, shall trigger termination of the onboarding process. The matter shall be escalated to the Principal Officer for assessment, including evaluation of whether the matter warrants suspicious transaction reporting under Section 15 of this Policy.

#### 10.3.5 Geo-Location Capture (Paragraph 4.2.5)

The Company's onboarding system shall be equipped to accurately capture the geo-location coordinates (latitude and longitude) of the client, in order to establish the precise location at which the verification process is undertaken.

In case of a mismatch between the address furnished by the client and the geo-coordinates captured during onboarding, enhanced measures under CDD shall be undertaken in accordance with Section 11.2 of this Policy.

#### 10.3.6 Penny-Drop Bank Account Verification (Paragraph 4.2.6)

Verification of the client's bank account shall be carried out through a penny-drop mechanism, for the purpose of confirming both ownership and operational status of the account.

The penny-drop mechanism involves a nominal amount being credited to the client's bank account, with verification of the account holder's name and account status returned by the receiving bank. The Company shall retain records of the penny-drop verification result, including timestamp, in the client's CDD file.
